name: New York City
summary: New York City is the biggest city in the United States, packed with skyscrapers, yellow taxis, and steaming hot dog carts. You can wave to the Statue of Liberty, zoom up the Empire State Building, and stand under giant T. rex bones at the American Museum of Natural History. At night, Times Square glows like a giant electric rainbow.
funFacts: The Statue of Liberty was a giant gift from France in 1886. She is made of copper, which is why the sun and rain turned her green.
The Empire State Building has 1,860 stairs and was the tallest building on Earth for 40 whole years before anything taller was built.
Central Park is bigger than the entire country of Monaco and has 21 playgrounds, a zoo, rowboats, and even sailboats kids can steer on a pond.
The New York City subway has 472 stations, more than any other subway in the world. Trains rumble underground 24 hours a day, every single day.
The American Museum of Natural History has around 100 dinosaurs on display, including a towering T. rex, and it inspired the Night at the Museum movies.
thingsToDo: Catch the ferry to Liberty Island and Ellis Island to stand beneath the giant green Statue of Liberty and learn how families arrived in America.
Zoom up the Empire State Building or One World Observatory in a super-fast lift to see Manhattan's whole skyline laid out below you.
Spend a morning in Central Park rowing boats at Loeb Boathouse, meeting goats at Tisch Children's Zoo, and racing along leafy paths.
Hunt for T. rex, Triceratops, and the enormous Titanosaur at the American Museum of Natural History, the real museum from Night at the Museum.
Wander through Times Square at night to see thousands of glowing billboards, costumed characters, and giant LED screens flashing all around you.
Walk across the Brooklyn Bridge from Manhattan to Brooklyn for amazing skyline photos, then grab a New York pizza slice on the other side.

localWord.meaning: A classic New York way of saying 'forget about it' — used when something is amazing, impossible, or just not worth worrying about.

images.alt: The Statue of Liberty stands tall on her island holding her torch up to the sky.
The famous Empire State Building rises high above the rooftops of New York City.
The Brooklyn Bridge stretches across the water with its big stone towers and crisscrossing cables.
Times Square glows at night with giant colorful billboards and bright neon lights everywhere.
The fairytale-like Belvedere Castle sits on a rocky hill in leafy Central Park.
